name: JW Player API
what_it_does: REST API and JavaScript player SDK for JW Player's video platform, covering video and playlist management, channel operations, analytics, and AI-powered recommendations.
use_cases: Manage and organize video libraries with metadata and playlists
Embed customizable HTML5 video player into web and mobile apps
Build OTT streaming apps with channel and live stream management
Retrieve video analytics for content performance insights
Implement AI-powered content recommendations for viewer retention
not_for: Teams needing full video encoding infrastructure without additional cost
Enterprise broadcast workflows requiring broadcast-grade SLAs
High-volume API usage without a corresponding plan
best_when: You are a media company, online course platform, or OTT service that needs a reliable video player with a solid management API and content recommendation capabilities.
avoid_when: You need a fully API-first developer experience without sales engagement, or need extensive SDK support across many languages.

auth.notes: REST API uses API key + API secret for HMAC-SHA1 signed requests; Management API uses v2 keys with different signing. Player embeds use site key.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter, Enterprise plans; pricing not fully public. Starter around $10/month for small publishers.
pricing.notes: Starter plan available with self-serve signup. Enterprise plans require sales contact. Bandwidth and storage limits vary by plan.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits apply; specific thresholds not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with status codes; v2 API has better error detail than legacy v1
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET and DELETE operations are idempotent; media uploads are not — can create duplicates
known_agent_gotchas: Two separate API versions (v1 and v2) with different auth mechanisms — can cause confusion
HMAC-SHA1 request signing is complex to implement correctly in agents
Domain whitelisting required for player embeds — affects testing environments
Analytics API has different base URL and auth from management API
Rate limits not publicly documented — implement conservative backoff
Credit card required at signup even for low-volume plans

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced, HMAC-signed requests, SOC2 Type II, GDPR compliant, domain whitelisting for players
